name: Radio
description: Single option within a mutually-exclusive group. Built on native input[type=radio]. Must be used inside a RadioGroup — RadioGroup owns the shared name, selected value, and change handler. A Radio outside a RadioGroup has no group semantics.

value.description: The value this radio represents. RadioGroup compares this against its selected value to determine checked state. Required.

size.description: Physical size. Usually inherited from RadioGroup; set individually only for unusual layouts.

disabled.description: Disables this specific radio option. The rest of the group remains interactive.

keyboard: Arrow keys move between options in the group
requirements: Radio must be inside a RadioGroup for correct keyboard navigation and group semantics
Each Radio needs an accessible label — via FormField.Label or aria-label
Only one Radio in a group can be selected at a time — enforced by RadioGroup
Arrow key navigation moves selection within the group (native radio behavior)

do: Always use Radio inside a RadioGroup
Give each Radio a unique value within its group
Use Radio when exactly one choice must be selected from a set
dont: Don't use Radio outside a RadioGroup — it loses group semantics and keyboard navigation
Don't use Radio for independent choices — that's Checkbox
Don't use Radio for on/off toggles — that's Switch

antiPatterns.why: Radios outside a RadioGroup have no shared name, no enforced single-selection, and no arrow-key navigation. RadioGroup provides all three.

use_instead_when.when: choices are independent (multiple can be selected)
there are many options and space is limited
